get.description:
"Establishes a WebSocket connection for real-time text-to-speech generation. This endpoint uses WebSocket protocol (wss://api.together.ai/v1/audio/speech/websocket) for bidirectional streaming communication.

**Connection Setup:**
- Protocol: WebSocket (wss://)
- Authentication: Pass API key as Bearer token in Authorization header
- Parameters: Sent as query parameters (model, voice, max_partial_length)

**Client Events:**
- `tts_session.updated`: Update session parameters like voice
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"tts_session.updated\",
    \"session\": {
      \"voice\": \"tara\"
    }
  }
  ```
- `input_text_buffer.append`: Send text chunks for TTS generation
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"input_text_buffer.append\",
    \"text\": \"Hello, this is a test.\"
  }
  ```
- `input_text_buffer.clear`: Clear the buffered text
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"input_text_buffer.clear\"
  }
  ```
- `input_text_buffer.commit`: Signal end of text input and process remaining text
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"input_text_buffer.commit\"
  }
  ```

**Server Events:**
- `session.created`: Initial session confirmation (sent first)
  ```json
  {
    \"event_id\": \"evt_123456\",
    \"type\": \"session.created\",
    \"session\": {
      \"id\": \"session-id\",
      \"object\": \"realtime.tts.session\",
      \"modalities\": [\"text\", \"audio\"],
      \"model\": \"hexgrad/Kokoro-82M\",
      \"voice\": \"tara\"
    }
  }
  ```
- `conversation.item.input_text.received`: Acknowledgment that text was received
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"conversation.item.input_text.received\",
    \"text\": \"Hello, this is a test.\"
  }
  ```
- `conversation.item.audio_output.delta`: Audio chunks as base64-encoded data
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"conversation.item.audio_output.delta\",
    \"item_id\": \"tts_1\",
    \"delta\": \"<base64_encoded_audio_chunk>\"
  }
  ```
- `conversation.item.audio_output.done`: Audio generation complete for an item
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"conversation.item.audio_output.done\",
    \"item_id\": \"tts_1\"
  }
  ```
- `conversation.item.tts.failed`: Error occurred
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"conversation.item.tts.failed\",
    \"error\": {
      \"message\": \"Error description\",
      \"type\": \"invalid_request_error\",
      \"param\": null,
      \"code\": \"invalid_api_key\"
    }
  }
  ```

**Text Processing:**
- Partial text (no sentence ending) is held in buffer until:
  - We believe that the text is complete enough to be processed for TTS generation
  - The partial text exceeds `max_partial_length` characters (default: 250)
  - The `input_text_buffer.commit` event is received

**Audio Format:**
- Format: WAV (PCM s16le)
- Sample Rate: 24000 Hz
- Encoding: Base64
- Delivered via `conversation.item.audio_output.delta` events

**Error Codes:**
- `invalid_api_key`: Invalid API key provided (401)
- `missing_api_key`: Authorization header missing (401)
- `model_not_available`: Invalid or unavailable model (400)
- Invalid text format errors (400)
"

schema.description: The TTS model to use for speech generation. Can also be set via `tts_session.updated` event.

The voice to use for speech generation. Default is 'tara'.
Available voices vary by model. Can also be updated via `tts_session.updated` event.

Maximum number of characters in partial text before forcing TTS generation
even without a sentence ending. Helps reduce latency for long text without punctuation.

get.description: List all GPU clusters.

post.description:
Create an Instant Cluster on Together's high-performance GPU clusters.
With features like on-demand scaling, long-lived resizable high-bandwidth shared DC-local storage,
Kubernetes and Slurm cluster flavors, a REST API, and Terraform support,
you can run workloads flexibly without complex infrastructure management.

get.summary: Get GPU cluster by cluster ID
get.description: Retrieve information about a specific GPU cluster.

schema.description: The ID of the cluster to retrieve

put.description: Update the configuration of an existing GPU cluster.

schema.description: The ID of the cluster to update

delete.summary: Delete GPU cluster by cluster ID
delete.description: Delete a GPU cluster by cluster ID.

schema.description: The ID of the cluster to delete

get.summary: List regions and corresponding supported driver versions

get.description: List all shared volumes.

put.description: Update the configuration of an existing shared volume.

post.description:
Instant Clusters supports long-lived, resizable in-DC shared storage with user data persistence.
You can dynamically create and attach volumes to your cluster at cluster creation time, and resize as your data grows.
All shared storage is backed by multi-NIC bare metal paths, ensuring high-throughput and low-latency performance for shared storage.

get.summary: Get shared volume by volume Id.
get.description: Retrieve information about a specific shared volume.

schema.description: The ID of the volume to retrieve

post.summary: Create a dedicated endpoint, it will start automatically
post.description: Creates a new dedicated endpoint for serving models. The endpoint will automatically start after creation. You can deploy any supported model on hardware configurations that meet the model's requirements.

schema.description: The ID of the endpoint to update

delete.description: Permanently deletes an endpoint. This action cannot be undone.

schema.description: The ID of the endpoint to delete

Filter hardware configurations by model compatibility. When provided,
the response includes availability status for each compatible configuration.
[See all of Together AI's dedicated models](https://docs.together.ai/docs/dedicated-models)

object.description: The object type, which is always `list`.

post.description:
Executes the given code snippet and returns the output. Without a session_id, a new session will be created to run the code. If you do pass in a valid session_id, the code will be run in that session. This is useful for running multiple code snippets in the same environment, because dependencies and similar things are persisted
between calls to the same session.

get.description:
"Establishes a WebSocket connection for real-time audio transcription. This endpoint uses WebSocket protocol (wss://api.together.ai/v1/realtime) for bidirectional streaming communication.

**Connection Setup:**
- Protocol: WebSocket (wss://)
- Authentication: Pass API key as Bearer token in Authorization header
- Parameters: Sent as query parameters (model, input_audio_format)

**Client Events:**
- `input_audio_buffer.append`: Send audio chunks as base64-encoded data
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"input_audio_buffer.append\",
    \"audio\": \"<base64_encoded_audio_chunk>\"
  }
  ```
- `input_audio_buffer.commit`: Signal end of audio stream
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"input_audio_buffer.commit\"
  }
  ```

**Server Events:**
- `session.created`: Initial session confirmation (sent first)
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"session.created\",
    \"session\": {
      \"id\": \"session-id\",
      \"object\": \"realtime.session\",
      \"modalities\": [\"audio\"],
      \"model\": \"openai/whisper-large-v3\"
    }
  }
  ```
- `conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.delta`: Partial transcription results
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.delta\",
    \"delta\": \"The quick brown\"
  }
  ```
- `conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.completed`: Final transcription
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.completed\",
    \"transcript\": \"The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog\"
  }
  ```
- `conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.failed`: Error occurred
  ```json
  {
    \"type\": \"conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.failed\",
    \"error\": {
      \"message\": \"Error description\",
      \"type\": \"invalid_request_error\",
      \"param\": null,
      \"code\": \"invalid_api_key\"
    }
  }
  ```

**Error Codes:**
- `invalid_api_key`: Invalid API key provided (401)
- `missing_api_key`: Authorization header missing (401)
- `model_not_available`: Invalid or unavailable model (400)
- Unsupported audio format errors (400)
"

schema.description: The Whisper model to use for transcription

parameters.name: input_audio_format
parameters.description: Audio format specification. Currently supports 16-bit PCM at 16kHz sample rate.

schema.title: DefaultResponse
schema.description: Default response schema

post.description:
Cancel a pending job. Only jobs in pending status can be canceled.
Running jobs cannot be stopped. Returns the job status after the
attempt. If the job is not pending, returns 409 with the current status
unchanged.

409.description: Job could not be canceled (already completed/failed)

get.description: Get the current queue statistics for a model, including pending and running job counts.

schema.description: Model name to get metrics for

get.description: Poll the current status of a previously submitted job. Provide the request_id and model as query parameters.

schema.description: Request ID returned from the submit endpoint
Model name the job was submitted to

post.description:
Submit a new job to the queue for asynchronous processing. Jobs are
processed in strict priority order (higher priority first, FIFO within
the same priority). Returns a request ID that can be used to poll status
or cancel the job.

region.description: Region to create the GPU cluster in. Usable regions can be found from `client.clusters.list_regions()`

gpu_type.description: Type of GPU to use in the cluster

num_gpus.description: Number of GPUs to allocate in the cluster. This must be multiple of 8. For example, 8, 16 or 24

duration_days.description: Duration in days to keep the cluster running.

volume_id.description: ID of an existing volume to use with the cluster creation.

billing_type.description:
RESERVED billing types allow you to specify the duration of the cluster reservation via the duration_days field.
ON_DEMAND billing types will give you ownership of the cluster until you delete it.

gpu_node_failover_enabled.description: Whether automated GPU node failover should be enabled for this cluster. By default, it is disabled.

auto_scaled.description: Whether GPU cluster should be auto-scaled based on the workload. By default, it is not auto-scaled.

auto_scale_max_gpus.description: Maximum number of GPUs to which the cluster can be auto-scaled up. This field is required if auto_scaled is true.

slurm_shm_size_gib.description: Shared memory size in GiB for Slurm cluster. This field is required if cluster_type is SLURM.

capacity_pool_id.description: ID of the capacity pool to use for the cluster. This field is optional and only applicable if the cluster is created from a capacity pool.

reservation_start_time.description: Reservation start time of the cluster. This field is required for SCHEDULED billing to specify the reservation start time for the cluster. If not provided, the cluster will be provisioned immediately.

reservation_end_time.description: Reservation end time of the cluster. This field is required for SCHEDULED billing to specify the reservation end time for the cluster.

install_traefik.description: Whether to install Traefik ingress controller in the cluster. This field is only applicable for Kubernetes clusters and is false by default.

cuda_version.description: CUDA version for this cluster. For example, 12.5

nvidia_driver_version.description: Nvidia driver version for this cluster. For example, 550. Only some combination of cuda_version and nvidia_driver_version are supported.

slurm_image.description: Custom Slurm image for Slurm clusters.

status.description: Current status of the GPU cluster.

num_gpus.description: Number of GPUs to allocate in the cluster. This must be multiple of 8. For example, 8, 16 or 24

reservation_end_time.description: Timestamp at which the cluster should be decommissioned. Only accepted for prepaid clusters.

driver_versions.description: List of supported identifiable cuda/nvidia driver versions pairs available in the region.

supported_instance_types.description: List of supported identifiable gpus available in the region.

ClusterDriverVersionInfo.description: CUDA/NVIDIA driver versions pair available in the region to use in the create cluster request.

volume_name.description: Customizable name of the volume to create.

region.description: Region name. Usable regions can be found from `client.clusters.list_regions()`

size_tib.description: Size of the volume in whole tebibytes (TiB).

volume_id.description: ID of the volume to update.

size_tib.description: Size of the volume in whole tebibytes (TiB).

ListVoicesResponse.description: Response containing a list of models and their available voices.

model.description: Model name.

name.description: Voice name to be used for audio inference.

voices.description: List of available voices for the model.

ModelVoices.description: Represents a model with its available voices.

model.description:
The model to be used for the rerank request.

[See all of Together AI's rerank models](https://docs.together.ai/docs/serverless-models#rerank-models)

query.description: The search query to be used for ranking.
query.example: What animals can I find near Peru?

documents.description: List of documents, which can be either strings or objects.

top_n.description: The number of top results to return.

return_documents.description: Whether to return supplied documents with the response.

rank_fields.description: List of keys in the JSON Object document to rank by. Defaults to use all supplied keys for ranking.

object.description: The object type, which is always `rerank`.

model.description: The model to be used for the rerank request.

TopLogprobs.description: Top log probabilities for the tokens.

token_ids.description: List of token IDs corresponding to the logprobs

top_logprobs.description: Top log probabilities for the tokens.

prompt.description: A string providing context for the model to complete.
prompt.example: <s>[INST] What is the capital of France? [/INST]

model.description:
The name of the model to query.

[See all of Together AI's chat models](https://docs.together.ai/docs/serverless-models#chat-models)

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate.

stop.description: A list of string sequences that will truncate (stop) inference text output. For example, "</s>" will stop generation as soon as the model generates the given token.

temperature.description: A decimal number from 0-1 that determines the degree of randomness in the response. A temperature less than 1 favors more correctness and is appropriate for question answering or summarization. A value closer to 1 introduces more randomness in the output.

top_p.description: A percentage (also called the nucleus parameter) that's used to dynamically adjust the number of choices for each predicted token based on the cumulative probabilities. It specifies a probability threshold below which all less likely tokens are filtered out. This technique helps maintain diversity and generate more fluent and natural-sounding text.

top_k.description: An integer that's used to limit the number of choices for the next predicted word or token. It specifies the maximum number of tokens to consider at each step, based on their probability of occurrence. This technique helps to speed up the generation process and can improve the quality of the generated text by focusing on the most likely options.

repetition_penalty.description: A number that controls the diversity of generated text by reducing the likelihood of repeated sequences. Higher values decrease repetition.

stream.description: "If true, stream tokens as Server-Sent Events as the model generates them instead of waiting for the full model response. The stream terminates with `data: [DONE]`. If false, return a single JSON object containing the results."

logprobs.description: An integer between 0 and 20 of the top k tokens to return log probabilities for at each generation step, instead of just the sampled token. Log probabilities help assess model confidence in token predictions.

echo.description: If true, the response will contain the prompt. Can be used with `logprobs` to return prompt logprobs.

n.description: The number of completions to generate for each prompt.

safety_model.description: The name of the moderation model used to validate tokens. Choose from the available moderation models found [here](https://docs.together.ai/docs/inference-models#moderation-models).

min_p.description: A number between 0 and 1 that can be used as an alternative to top-p and top-k.

presence_penalty.description: A number between -2.0 and 2.0 where a positive value increases the likelihood of a model talking about new topics.

frequency_penalty.description: A number between -2.0 and 2.0 where a positive value decreases the likelihood of repeating tokens that have already been mentioned.

logit_bias.description: Adjusts the likelihood of specific tokens appearing in the generated output.

object.description: The object type, which is always `text.completion`.
The object type, which is always `completion.chunk`.

messages.description: A list of messages comprising the conversation so far.

model.description:
The name of the model to query.

[See all of Together AI's chat models](https://docs.together.ai/docs/serverless-models#chat-models)

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate.

stop.description: A list of string sequences that will truncate (stop) inference text output. For example, "</s>" will stop generation as soon as the model generates the given token.

temperature.description: A decimal number from 0-1 that determines the degree of randomness in the response. A temperature less than 1 favors more correctness and is appropriate for question answering or summarization. A value closer to 1 introduces more randomness in the output.

top_p.description: A percentage (also called the nucleus parameter) that's used to dynamically adjust the number of choices for each predicted token based on the cumulative probabilities. It specifies a probability threshold below which all less likely tokens are filtered out. This technique helps maintain diversity and generate more fluent and natural-sounding text.

top_k.description: An integer that's used to limit the number of choices for the next predicted word or token. It specifies the maximum number of tokens to consider at each step, based on their probability of occurrence. This technique helps to speed up the generation process and can improve the quality of the generated text by focusing on the most likely options.

context_length_exceeded_behavior.description: Defined the behavior of the API when max_tokens exceed the maximum context length of the model. When set to 'error', API will return 400 with appropriate error message. When set to 'truncate', override the max_tokens with maximum context length of the model.

repetition_penalty.description: A number that controls the diversity of generated text by reducing the likelihood of repeated sequences. Higher values decrease repetition.

stream.description: "If true, stream tokens as Server-Sent Events as the model generates them instead of waiting for the full model response. The stream terminates with `data: [DONE]`. If false, return a single JSON object containing the results."

logprobs.description: An integer between 0 and 20 of the top k tokens to return log probabilities for at each generation step, instead of just the sampled token. Log probabilities help assess model confidence in token predictions.

echo.description: If true, the response will contain the prompt. Can be used with `logprobs` to return prompt logprobs.

n.description: The number of completions to generate for each prompt.

min_p.description: A number between 0 and 1 that can be used as an alternative to top_p and top-k.

presence_penalty.description: A number between -2.0 and 2.0 where a positive value increases the likelihood of a model talking about new topics.

frequency_penalty.description: A number between -2.0 and 2.0 where a positive value decreases the likelihood of repeating tokens that have already been mentioned.

logit_bias.description: Adjusts the likelihood of specific tokens appearing in the generated output.

response_format.description:
"An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Setting to `{ \"type\": \"json_schema\", \"json_schema\": {...} }` enables
Structured Outputs which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON
schema. Learn more in the [Structured Outputs
guide](https://docs.together.ai/docs/json-mode).

Setting to `{ \"type\": \"json_object\" }` enables the older JSON mode, which
ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using `json_schema`
is preferred for models that support it.
"

tools.description: A list of tools the model may call. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool. Use this to provide a list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for.

tool_choice.description: Controls which (if any) function is called by the model. By default uses `auto`, which lets the model pick between generating a message or calling a function.

chat_template_kwargs.description: Additional configuration to pass to model engine.

safety_model.description: The name of the moderation model used to validate tokens. Choose from the available moderation models found [here](https://docs.together.ai/docs/inference-models#moderation-models).

reasoning_effort.description: Controls the level of reasoning effort the model should apply when generating responses. Higher values may result in more thoughtful and detailed responses but may take longer to generate.

ResponseFormatText.title: Text

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `text`.

ResponseFormatText.description: Default response format. Used to generate text responses.

ResponseFormatJsonObject.title: JSON object

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_object`.

ResponseFormatJsonObject.description:
JSON object response format. An older method of generating JSON responses.
Using `json_schema` is recommended for models that support it. Note that the
model will not generate JSON without a system or user message instructing it
to do so.

ResponseFormatJsonSchema.title: JSON schema

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

ResponseFormatJsonSchema.description:
JSON Schema response format. Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](https://docs.together.ai/docs/json-mode).

ResponseFormatJsonSchemaSchema.title: JSON schema
ResponseFormatJsonSchemaSchema.description:
The schema for the response format, described as a JSON Schema object.
Learn how to build JSON schemas [here](https://json-schema.org/).

RLForwardBackwardResultMetrics.description: Loss-specific metrics (e.g., KL divergence, clip fraction for GRPO)

format.description: The format of the audio data

ChatCompletionUserMessageContentMultimod.description: A structured message with mixed content types.

ChatCompletionUserMessageContent.description: The content of the message, which can either be a simple string or a structured format.

text.example: <s>[INST] What is the capital of France? [/INST]

object.description: The object type, which is always `chat.completion`.
The object type, which is always `chat.completion.chunk`.

top_logprobs.description: Top log probabilities for the tokens.

model.description:
"The name of the model to query.

[See all of Together AI's chat models](https://docs.together.ai/docs/serverless-models#audio-models) The current supported tts models are: - cartesia/sonic - hexgrad/Kokoro-82M - canopylabs/orpheus-3b-0.1-ft "

input.description: Input text to generate the audio for

voice.description:
The voice to use for generating the audio. The voices supported are different for each model. For eg - for canopylabs/orpheus-3b-0.1-ft, one of the voices supported is tara, for hexgrad/Kokoro-82M, one of the voices supported is af_alloy and for cartesia/sonic, one of the voices supported is "friendly sidekick".

You can view the voices supported for each model using the /v1/voices endpoint sending the model name as the query parameter. [View all supported voices here](https://docs.together.ai/docs/text-to-speech#supported-voices).

response_format.description: The format of audio output. Supported formats are mp3, wav, raw if streaming is false. If streaming is true, the only supported format is raw.

sample_rate.description: Sampling rate to use for the output audio. The default sampling rate for canopylabs/orpheus-3b-0.1-ft and hexgrad/Kokoro-82M is 24000 and for cartesia/sonic is 44100.

bit_rate.description: Bitrate of the MP3 audio output in bits per second. Only applicable when response_format is mp3. Higher values produce better audio quality at larger file sizes. Default is 128000. Currently supported on Cartesia models.

stream.description: "If true, output is streamed for several characters at a time instead of waiting for the full response. The stream terminates with `data: [DONE]`. If false, return the encoded audio as octet stream"

file.description: Audio file upload or public HTTP/HTTPS URL. Supported formats .wav, .mp3, .m4a, .webm, .flac.

model.description: Model to use for transcription

language.description: Optional ISO 639-1 language code. If `auto` is provided, language is auto-detected.

timestamp_granularities.description: Controls level of timestamp detail in verbose_json. Only used when response_format is verbose_json. Can be a single granularity or an array to get multiple levels.

diarize.description:
"Whether to enable speaker diarization. When enabled, you will get the speaker id for each word in the transcription. In the response, in the words array, you will get the speaker id for each word. In addition, we also return the speaker_segments array which contains the speaker id for each speaker segment along with the start and end time of the segment along with all the words in the segment.

For eg - ... \"speaker_segments\": [ \"speaker_id\": \"SPEAKER_00\", \"start\": 0, \"end\": 30.02, \"words\": [ { \"id\": 0, \"word\": \"Tijana\", \"start\": 0, \"end\": 11.475, \"speaker_id\": \"SPEAKER_00\" }, ... "

min_speakers.description: Minimum number of speakers expected in the audio. Used to improve diarization accuracy when the approximate number of speakers is known.

max_speakers.description: Maximum number of speakers expected in the audio. Used to improve diarization accuracy when the approximate number of speakers is known.

duration.description: The duration of the audio in seconds

words.description: Array of transcription words (only when timestamp_granularities includes 'word')

speaker_segments.description: Array of transcription speaker segments (only when diarize is enabled)

start.description: Start time of the segment in seconds

end.description: End time of the segment in seconds

text.description: The text content of the segment

start.description: Start time of the word in seconds

end.description: End time of the word in seconds

speaker_id.description: The speaker id for the word (only when diarize is enabled)

start.description: Start time of the speaker segment in seconds

end.description: End time of the speaker segment in seconds

words.description: Array of words spoken by this speaker in this segment

text.description: The full text spoken by this speaker in this segment
text.example: Hello, how are you doing today?

id.description: Unique identifier for the speaker segment

file.description: Audio file upload or public HTTP/HTTPS URL. Supported formats .wav, .mp3, .m4a, .webm, .flac.

model.description: Model to use for translation

language.description: Target output language. Optional ISO 639-1 language code. If omitted, language is set to English.

timestamp_granularities.description: Controls level of timestamp detail in verbose_json. Only used when response_format is verbose_json. Can be a single granularity or an array to get multiple levels.

language.description: The target language of the translation

duration.description: The duration of the audio in seconds

words.description: Array of translation words (only when timestamp_granularities includes 'word')

object.description: The object type, which is always `audio.tts.chunk`.

model.description:
The name of the embedding model to use.

[See all of Together AI's embedding models](https://docs.together.ai/docs/serverless-models#embedding-models)

input.example: Our solar system orbits the Milky Way galaxy at about 515,000 mph

object.description: The object type, which is always `list`.
The object type, which is always `embedding`.
The object type, which is always `model`.

model_name.description: The name to give to your uploaded model

model_source.description: The source location of the model (Hugging Face repo or S3 path)

model_type.description: Whether the model is a full model or an adapter

hf_token.description: Hugging Face token (if uploading from Hugging Face)

base_model.description: The base model to use for an adapter if setting it to run against a serverless pool.  Only used for model_type `adapter`.

lora_model.description: The lora pool to use for an adapter if setting it to run against, say, a dedicated pool.  Only used for model_type `adapter`.

object.description: The object type, which is always `list`.
The object type, which is always `file`.

created_at.description: The timestamp when the file was created.

filename.description: The name of the file as it was uploaded.

bytes.description: The number of bytes in the file.

Processed.description: Whether the file has been parsed and analyzed for correctness for fine-tuning.

FileResponse.description: Structured information describing a file uploaded to Together.

id.description: Unique identifier for the fine-tune job

created_at.description: Creation timestamp of the fine-tune job

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp of the fine-tune job

started_at.description: Start timestamp of the current stage of the fine-tune job

user_id.description: Identifier for the user who created the job

total_price.description: Total price for the fine-tuning job

events.description: Events related to this fine-tune job

packing.description: Whether sequence packing is being used for training.

model.description: Base model used for fine-tuning

suffix.description: Suffix added to the fine-tuned model name

n_checkpoints.description: Number of checkpoints saved during training

training_type.description: Type of training used (full or LoRA)

random_seed.description: Random seed used for training. Integer when set; null if not stored (e.g. legacy jobs) or no explicit seed was recorded.

from_hf_model.description: Hugging Face Hub repo to start training from

hf_model_revision.description: The revision of the Hugging Face Hub model to continue training from

FinetuneResponseTruncated.description: A truncated version of the fine-tune response, used for POST /fine-tunes, GET /fine-tunes and POST /fine-tunes/{id}/cancel endpoints

message.description: Message indicating the result of the deletion

supports_vision.description: Whether the model supports vision/multimodal inputs.

supports_tools.description: Whether the model supports tool/function calling.

merge_output_lora.description: Whether to merge the output LoRA.

object.description: The object type, which is always `fine-tune-event`.

seconds_remaining.description: Estimated time remaining in seconds for the fine-tuning job to next state

FineTuneProgress.description: Progress information for a fine-tuning job

train_on_inputs.description: Whether to mask the user messages in conversational data or prompts in instruction data.

train_vision.description: Whether to train the vision encoder of the model. Only available for multimodal models.

min_lr_ratio.description: The ratio of the final learning rate to the peak learning rate

num_cycles.description: Number or fraction of cycles for the cosine learning rate scheduler

min_lr_ratio.description: The ratio of the final learning rate to the peak learning rate

min_replicas.description: The minimum number of replicas to maintain, even when there is no load

max_replicas.description: The maximum number of replicas to scale up to under load

Autoscaling.description: Configuration for automatic scaling of replicas based on demand.

gpu_memory.description: Amount of GPU memory in GB

gpu_count.description: Number of GPUs in this configuration

cents_per_minute.description: Cost per minute of endpoint uptime in cents

status.description: The availability status of the hardware configuration

HardwareAvailability.description: Indicates the current availability status of a hardware configuration

updated_at.description: Timestamp of when the hardware status was last updated

HardwareWithStatus.description: Hardware configuration details with optional availability status

display_name.description: A human-readable name for the endpoint

model.description: The model to deploy on this endpoint

hardware.description: The hardware configuration to use for this endpoint

disable_prompt_cache.description: This parameter is deprecated and no longer has any effect.

disable_speculative_decoding.description: Whether to disable speculative decoding for this endpoint

state.description: The desired state of the endpoint

inactive_timeout.description: The number of minutes of inactivity after which the endpoint will be automatically stopped. Set to null, omit or set to 0 to disable automatic timeout.

availability_zone.description: Create the endpoint in a specified availability zone (e.g., us-central-4b)

ErrorOutput.title: ErrorOutput
ErrorOutput.description: Errors and exceptions that occurred. If this output type is present, your code did not execute successfully.

encoding.description: Encoding of the file content. Use `string` for text files such as code, and `base64` for binary files, such as images.

files.description: Files to upload to the session. If present, files will be uploaded before executing the given code.

language.description: Programming language for the code to execute. Currently only supports Python, but more will be added.

session_id.description: Identifier of the current session. Used to make follow-up calls. Requests will return an error if the session does not belong to the caller or has expired.

ExecuteResponse.title: ExecuteResponse

oneOf.title: StreamOutput
oneOf.description: Outputs that were printed to stdout or stderr

oneOf.title: ErrorOutput
oneOf.description: Errors and exceptions that occurred. If this output type is present, your code did not execute successfully.

session_id.description: Identifier of the current session. Used to make follow-up calls.

status.description: Status of the execution. Currently only supports success.

ExecuteResponse.description: The result of the execution. If successful, `data` contains the result and `errors` will be null. If unsuccessful, `data` will be null and `errors` will contain the errors.

oneOf.title: StreamOutput
oneOf.description: Outputs that were printed to stdout or stderr

oneOf.title: ErrorOutput
oneOf.description: Errors and exceptions that occurred. If this output type is present, your code did not execute successfully.

id.description: Session Identifier. Used to make follow-up calls.

StreamOutput.title: StreamOutput
StreamOutput.description: Outputs that were printed to stdout or stderr

endpoint.description: The endpoint to use for batch processing

input_file_id.description: ID of the uploaded input file containing batch requests

model_id.description: Model to use for processing batch requests

file_size_bytes.description: Size of input file in bytes

BatchJobStatus.description: Current status of the batch job

type.description: The type of evaluation to perform

pass_labels.description: List of labels that are considered passing

model.description: Name of the judge model

system_template.description: System prompt template for the judge
system_template.example: Imagine you are a helpful assistant

external_api_token.description: Bearer/API token for external judge models.

external_base_url.description: Base URL for external judge models. Must be OpenAI-compatible base URL.

num_workers.description: Number of concurrent workers for inference requests. Overrides the default concurrency for this model. Useful for tuning throughput when using proxy endpoints (e.g. OpenRouter) or rate-limited external APIs.

model.description: Name of the model to evaluate

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

input_template.example: Please classify {{prompt}} based on the labels below

external_base_url.description: Base URL for external models. Must be OpenAI-compatible base URL

num_workers.description: Number of concurrent workers for inference requests. Overrides the default concurrency for this model. Useful for tuning throughput when using proxy endpoints (e.g. OpenRouter) or rate-limited external APIs.

workflow_id.description: The ID of the created evaluation job

owner_id.description: ID of the job owner (admin only)

parameters.description: The parameters used for this evaluation

updated_at.description: When the job was last updated

invalid_score_count.description: number of invalid scores generated from model

failed_samples.description: number of failed samples generated from model

A_wins.description: Number of times model A won

B_wins.description: Number of times model B won

top_logprobs.description: Top log probabilities for the tokens.

text.example: The capital of France is Paris. It's located in the north-central part of the country and is one of the most populous and visited cities in the world, known for its iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower, Louvre Museum, Notre-Dame Cathedral, and more. Paris is also the capital of the Île-de-France region and is a major global center for art, fashion, gastronomy, and culture.

CreateVideoBody.title: Create video request

model.description: The model to be used for the video creation request.

prompt.description: Text prompt that describes the video to generate.

steps.description: The number of denoising steps the model performs during video generation. More steps typically result in higher quality output but require longer processing time.

seed.description: Seed to use in initializing the video generation.  Using the same seed allows deterministic video generation.  If not provided a random seed is generated for each request.

guidance_scale.description: Controls how closely the video generation follows your prompt. Higher values make the model adhere more strictly to your text description, while lower values allow more creative freedom. guidence_scale affects both visual content and temporal consistency.Recommended range is 6.0-10.0 for most video models. Values above 12 may cause over-guidance artifacts or unnatural motion patterns.

negative_prompt.description: Similar to prompt, but specifies what to avoid instead of what to include

generate_audio.description: Whether to generate audio for the video.

frame_images.description: "Deprecated: use media.frame_images instead. Array of images to guide video generation, similar to keyframes."

reference_images.description: "Deprecated: use media.reference_images instead. Unlike frame_images which constrain specific timeline positions, reference images guide the general appearance that should appear consistently across the video."

CreateVideoBody.description: Parameters for creating a new video generation job.

VideoStatus.description: Current lifecycle status of the video job.

input_image.description: URL path to hosted image that is used for a frame

frame.description:
Optional param to specify where to insert the frame. If this is omitted, the following heuristics are applied:
- frame_images size is one, frame is first.
- If size is two, frames are first and last.
- If size is larger, frames are first, last and evenly spaced between.

frame_images.description: Array of images to guide video generation at specific timeline positions.

frame_videos.description: Array of video clips to use as starting clips.

reference_images.description: Array of image URLs that guide the general appearance across the video.

source_video.description: Source video to edit. Accepts a URL string or an object with a "video" key.

audio_inputs.description: Array of audio inputs. Each element accepts a URL string or an object with an "audio" key.

VideoMedia.description: Contains all media inputs for video generation. Accepted fields depend on the model type.

VideoJob.title: Video job

id.description: Unique identifier for the video job.

object.description: The object type, which is always video.

model.description: The video generation model that produced the job.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) for when the job was created.

completed_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) for when the job completed, if finished.

size.description: The resolution of the generated video.

seconds.description: Duration of the generated clip in seconds.

VideoJob.description: Structured information describing a generated video job.

finishedAt.description: FinishedAt is the timestamp when the container finished execution (if terminated)

message.description: Message provides a human-readable message with details about the container's status

name.description: Name is the name of the container

reason.description: Reason provides a brief machine-readable reason for the container's current status

startedAt.description: StartedAt is the timestamp when the container started execution

status.description: Status is the current state of the container (e.g., "Running", "Terminated", "Waiting")

args.description: Args overrides the container's CMD. Provide as an array of arguments (e.g., ["python", "app.py"])

autoscaling.description: "Autoscaling configuration. Example: {\"metric\": \"QueueBacklogPerWorker\", \"target\": 1.01} to scale based on queue backlog. Omit or set to null to disable autoscaling"

command.description: Command overrides the container's ENTRYPOINT. Provide as an array (e.g., ["/bin/sh", "-c"])

cpu.description: CPU is the number of CPU cores to allocate per container instance (e.g., 0.1 = 100 milli cores)

description.description: Description is an optional human-readable description of your deployment

environment_variables.description: EnvironmentVariables is a list of environment variables to set in the container. Each must have a name and either a value or value_from_secret

gpu_count.description: GPUCount is the number of GPUs to allocate per container instance. Defaults to 0 if not specified

gpu_type.description: GPUType specifies the GPU hardware to use (e.g., "h100-80gb").

health_check_path.description: HealthCheckPath is the HTTP path for health checks (e.g., "/health"). If set, the platform will check this endpoint to determine container health

image.description: Image is the container image to deploy from registry.together.ai.

max_replicas.description: MaxReplicas is the maximum number of container instances that can be scaled up to. If not set, will be set to MinReplicas

memory.description: Memory is the amount of RAM to allocate per container instance in GiB (e.g., 0.5 = 512MiB)

min_replicas.description: MinReplicas is the minimum number of container instances to run. Defaults to 1 if not specified

name.description: Name is the unique identifier for your deployment. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, underscores, or hyphens (1-100 characters)

port.description: Port is the container port your application listens on (e.g., 8080 for web servers). Required if your application serves traffic

storage.description: Storage is the amount of ephemeral disk storage to allocate per container instance (e.g., 10 = 10GiB)

termination_grace_period_seconds.description: TerminationGracePeriodSeconds is the time in seconds to wait for graceful shutdown before forcefully terminating the replica

volumes.description: Volumes is a list of volume mounts to attach to the container. Each mount must reference an existing volume by name

description.description: Description is an optional human-readable description of the secret's purpose (max 500 characters)

name.description: Name is the unique identifier for the secret. Can contain alphanumeric characters, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, and periods (1-100 characters)

project_id.description: ProjectID is ignored - the project is automatically determined from your authentication

value.description: Value is the sensitive data to store securely (e.g., API keys, passwords, tokens). This value will be encrypted at rest

content.description: Content specifies the content configuration for this volume

name.description: Name is the unique identifier for the volume within the project

data.description: Data is the array of deployment items

object.description: The object type, which is always `list`.

args.description: Args are the arguments passed to the container's command

autoscaling.description: Autoscaling contains autoscaling configuration parameters for this deployment. Omitted when autoscaling is disabled (nil)

cpu.description: CPU is the amount of CPU resource allocated to each replica in cores (fractional value is allowed)

description.description: Description provides a human-readable explanation of the deployment's purpose or content

desired_replicas.description: DesiredReplicas is the number of replicas that the orchestrator is targeting

environment_variables.description: EnvironmentVariables is a list of environment variables set in the container

gpu_count.description: GPUCount is the number of GPUs allocated to each replica in this deployment

gpu_type.description: GPUType specifies the type of GPU requested (if any) for this deployment

health_check_path.description: HealthCheckPath is the HTTP path used for health checks of the application

name.description: Name is the name of the deployment

port.description: Port is the container port that the deployment exposes

ready_replicas.description: ReadyReplicas is the current number of replicas that are in the Ready state

replica_events.description: ReplicaEvents is a mapping of replica names or IDs to their status events

status.description: Status represents the overall status of the deployment (e.g., Updating, Scaling, Ready, Failed)

storage.description: Storage is the amount of storage (in MB or units as defined by the platform) allocated to each replica

updated_at.description: UpdatedAt is the ISO8601 timestamp when this deployment was last updated

volumes.description: Volumes is a list of volume mounts for this deployment

name.description: Name is the environment variable name (e.g., "DATABASE_URL"). Must start with a letter or underscore, followed by letters, numbers, or underscores

value.description: Value is the plain text value for the environment variable. Use this for non-sensitive values. Either Value or ValueFromSecret must be set, but not both

value_from_secret.description: ValueFromSecret references a secret by name or ID to use as the value. Use this for sensitive values like API keys or passwords. Either Value or ValueFromSecret must be set, but not both

metric.description: Metric must be HTTPTotalRequests or HTTPAvgRequestDuration

target.description: "Target is the threshold value. Default: 100 for HTTPTotalRequests, 500 (ms) for HTTPAvgRequestDuration"

time_interval_minutes.description: "TimeIntervalMinutes is the rate window in minutes. Default: 10"

model.description: Model overrides the model name for queue status lookup. Defaults to the deployment app name

custom_metric_name.description: CustomMetricName is the Prometheus metric name. Required. Must match [a-zA-Z_:][a-zA-Z0-9_:]*

action.description: Action is the action taken or reported by this event

count.description: Count is the number of times this event has occurred

first_seen.description: FirstSeen is the timestamp when this event was first observed

last_seen.description: LastSeen is the timestamp when this event was last observed

message.description: Message is a human-readable description of the event

reason.description: Reason is a brief machine-readable reason for this event (e.g., "Pulling", "Started", "Failed")

replica_status_reason.description: ReplicaStatusReason provides a brief machine-readable reason for the replica's status

command.description: Command overrides the container's ENTRYPOINT. Provide as an array (e.g., ["/bin/sh", "-c"])

memory.description: Memory is the amount of RAM to allocate per container instance in GiB (e.g., 0.5 = 512MiB)

min_replicas.description: MinReplicas is the minimum number of replicas to run

name.description: Name is the new unique identifier for your deployment. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, underscores, or hyphens (1-100 characters)

port.description: Port is the container port your application listens on (e.g., 8080 for web servers)

storage.description: Storage is the amount of ephemeral disk storage to allocate per container instance (e.g., 10 = 10GiB)

termination_grace_period_seconds.description: TerminationGracePeriodSeconds is the time in seconds to wait for graceful shutdown before forcefully terminating the replica

volumes.description: Volumes is a list of volume mounts to attach to the container. This will replace all existing volumes

description.description: Description is an optional human-readable description of the secret's purpose (max 500 characters)

name.description: Name is the new unique identifier for the secret. Can contain alphanumeric characters, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, and periods (1-100 characters)

project_id.description: ProjectID is ignored - the project is automatically determined from your authentication

value.description: Value is the new sensitive data to store securely. Updating this will replace the existing secret value

content.description: Content specifies the new content that will be preloaded to this volume

name.description: Name is the new unique identifier for the volume within the project

mount_path.description: MountPath is the path in the container where the volume will be mounted (e.g., "/data")

name.description: Name is the name of the volume to mount. Must reference an existing volume by name or ID

version.description: Version is the volume version to mount. On create, defaults to the latest version. On update, defaults to the currently mounted version.

created_at.description: CreatedAt is the ISO8601 timestamp when this volume was created

current_version.description: CurrentVersion is the current version number of this volume

id.description: ID is the unique identifier for this volume

mounted_by.description: MountedBy is the list of deployment IDs currently mounting current volume version

name.description: Name is the name of the volume

object.description: Object is the type identifier for this response (always "volume")

updated_at.description: UpdatedAt is the ISO8601 timestamp when this volume was last updated

version_history.description: VersionHistory contains previous versions of this volume, keyed by version number

source_prefix.description: SourcePrefix is the file path prefix for the content to be preloaded into the volume

type.description: Type is the content type (currently only "files" is supported which allows preloading files uploaded via Files API into the volume)

VolumeContentRequest.description: Content specifies the new content that will be preloaded to this volume

files.description: Files is the list of files that will be preloaded into the volume, if the volume content type is "files"

source_prefix.description: SourcePrefix is the file path prefix for the content to be preloaded into the volume

type.description: Type is the content type (currently only "files" is supported which allows preloading files uploaded via Files API into the volume)

last_modified.description: LastModified is the timestamp when the file was last modified

name.description: Name is the filename including extension (e.g., "model_weights.bin")

size.description: Size is the file size in bytes

param.description: The parameter that caused the error, if applicable

messages_running.description: Number of jobs currently being processed

messages_waiting.description: Number of jobs waiting to be claimed by a worker

total_jobs.description: Total number of active jobs (waiting + running)

model.description: Model identifier the job was submitted to

request_id.description: The request ID returned from the submit endpoint

status.description:
Job status after the cancel attempt. Only pending jobs can be
canceled. If the job is already running, done, or failed, the status
is returned unchanged.

info.description:
Arbitrary JSON metadata stored with the job and returned in status
responses. The model and system may add or update keys during
processing.

model.description: Required model identifier

payload.description: Freeform model input. Passed unchanged to the model. Contents are model-specific.

priority.description:
Job priority. Higher values are processed first (strict priority
ordering). Jobs with equal priority are processed in submission
order (FIFO).

requestId.description: Unique identifier for the submitted job. Use this to poll status or cancel.

claimed_at.description: Timestamp when a worker claimed the job

created_at.description: Timestamp when the job was created

done_at.description: Timestamp when the job completed (done or failed)

info.description:
Job metadata. Contains keys from the submit request, plus any
modifications from the model or system (e.g. progress, retry
history).

model.description: Model identifier the job was submitted to

outputs.description: Freeform model output, populated when the job reaches done status. Contents are model-specific.

priority.description: Job priority. Higher values are processed first.

request_id.description: The request ID that was returned from the submit endpoint

retries.description:
Number of times this job has been retried. Workers set a claim
timeout and must send periodic status updates to keep the job alive.
If no update is received within the timeout, the job is returned to
the queue and retried. After 3 retries the job is permanently
failed. Jobs explicitly failed by the model are not retried.

status.description:
"Current job status. Transitions: pending → running → done/failed. A pending job may also be canceled.
"

warnings.description: Non-fatal messages about the request (e.g. deprecation notices)

CompletionRequestLogitBias.description: Adjusts the likelihood of specific tokens appearing in the generated output.

ChatCompletionRequestLogitBias.description: Adjusts the likelihood of specific tokens appearing in the generated output.

ChatCompletionRequestReasoning.description: For models that support toggling reasoning functionality, this object can be used to control that functionality.

ResponseFormatJsonSchemaJsonSchema.title: JSON schema

description.description:
A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to
determine how to respond in the format.

name.description:
The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain
underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

schema.description:
The schema for the response format, described as a JSON Schema object.
Learn how to build JSON schemas [here](https://json-schema.org/).

ResponseFormatJsonSchemaJsonSchema.description: Structured Outputs configuration options, including a JSON Schema.

parameters.description: A map of parameter names to their values.

max_batch_size.description: Maximum batch size for SFT full training.

max_batch_size_dpo.description: Maximum batch size for DPO full training.

min_batch_size.description: Minimum batch size for full training.

max_batch_size.description: Maximum batch size for SFT LoRA training.

max_batch_size_dpo.description: Maximum batch size for DPO LoRA training.

min_batch_size.description: Minimum batch size for LoRA training.

VideoJobError.description: Error payload that explains why generation failed, if applicable.

cost.description: The cost of generated video charged to the owners account.

VideoJobOutputs.description: Available upon completion, the outputs provides the cost charged and the hosted url to access the video

prompt.description: A description of the desired images. Maximum length varies by model.

model.description:
The model to use for image generation.

[See all of Together AI's image models](https://docs.together.ai/docs/serverless-models#image-models)

image_url.description: URL of an image to use for image models that support it.

seed.description: Seed used for generation. Can be used to reproduce image generations.

n.description: Number of image results to generate.

height.description: Height of the image to generate in number of pixels.

width.description: Width of the image to generate in number of pixels.

negative_prompt.description: The prompt or prompts not to guide the image generation.

response_format.description: Format of the image response. Can be either a base64 string or a URL.

guidance_scale.description: Adjusts the alignment of the generated image with the input prompt. Higher values (e.g., 8-10) make the output more faithful to the prompt, while lower values (e.g., 1-5) encourage more creative freedom.

output_format.description: The format of the image response. Can be either be `jpeg` or `png`. Defaults to `jpeg`.

path.description: The URL of the LoRA to apply (e.g. https://huggingface.co/strangerzonehf/Flux-Midjourney-Mix2-LoRA).

scale.description: The strength of the LoRA's influence. Most LoRA's recommend a value of 1.

image_loras.description: An array of objects that define LoRAs (Low-Rank Adaptations) to influence the generated image.

items.description: URL of a reference image to guide the image generation.

reference_images.description: An array of image URLs that guide the overall appearance and style of the generated image. These reference images influence the visual characteristics consistently across the generation.

disable_safety_checker.description: If true, disables the safety checker for image generation.

file_name.description: The name of the file being uploaded

file.description: The content of the file being uploaded

training_file.description: File-ID of a training file uploaded to the Together API

validation_file.description: File-ID of a validation file uploaded to the Together API

packing.description: Whether to use sequence packing for training.

model.description: Name of the base model to run fine-tune job on

n_epochs.description: Number of complete passes through the training dataset (higher values may improve results but increase cost and risk of overfitting)

n_checkpoints.description: Number of intermediate model versions saved during training for evaluation

n_evals.description: Number of evaluations to be run on a given validation set during training

batch_size.description: Number of training examples processed together (larger batches use more memory but may train faster). Defaults to "max". We use training optimizations like packing, so the effective batch size may be different than the value you set.

learning_rate.description: Controls how quickly the model adapts to new information (too high may cause instability, too low may slow convergence)

warmup_ratio.description: The percent of steps at the start of training to linearly increase the learning rate.

max_grad_norm.description: Max gradient norm to be used for gradient clipping. Set to 0 to disable.

weight_decay.description: Weight decay. Regularization parameter for the optimizer.

random_seed.description: Random seed for reproducible training. When set, the same seed produces the same run (e.g. data shuffle, init). If omitted or null, the server applies its default seed (e.g. 42).

suffix.description: Suffix that will be added to your fine-tuned model name

wandb_api_key.description: Integration key for tracking experiments and model metrics on W&B platform

wandb_base_url.description: The base URL of a dedicated Weights & Biases instance.

wandb_project_name.description: The Weights & Biases project for your run. If not specified, will use `together` as the project name.

wandb_name.description: The Weights & Biases name for your run.

wandb_entity.description: The Weights & Biases entity for your run.

train_on_inputs.description: Whether to mask the user messages in conversational data or prompts in instruction data.

training_method.description: The training method to use. 'sft' for Supervised Fine-Tuning or 'dpo' for Direct Preference Optimization.

training_type.description: The training type to use. If not provided, the job will default to LoRA training type.

from_checkpoint.description: The checkpoint identifier to continue training from a previous fine-tuning job. Format is `{$JOB_ID}` or `{$OUTPUT_MODEL_NAME}` or `{$JOB_ID}:{$STEP}` or `{$OUTPUT_MODEL_NAME}:{$STEP}`. The step value is optional; without it, the final checkpoint will be used.

from_hf_model.description: The Hugging Face Hub repo to start training from. Should be as close as possible to the base model (specified by the `model` argument) in terms of architecture and size.

hf_model_revision.description: The revision of the Hugging Face Hub model to continue training from. E.g., hf_model_revision=main (default, used if the argument is not provided) or hf_model_revision='607a30d783dfa663caf39e06633721c8d4cfcd7e' (specific commit).

hf_api_token.description: The API token for the Hugging Face Hub.

hf_output_repo_name.description: The name of the Hugging Face repository to upload the fine-tuned model to.

training_file.description: File-ID of a training file uploaded to the Together API

validation_file.description: File-ID of a validation file uploaded to the Together API

model.description: Name of the base model to run fine-tune job on

n_epochs.description: Number of complete passes through the training dataset (higher values may improve results but increase cost and risk of overfitting)

n_evals.description: Number of evaluations to be run on a given validation set during training

training_method.description: The training method to use. 'sft' for Supervised Fine-Tuning or 'dpo' for Direct Preference Optimization.

training_type.description: The training type to use. If not provided, the job will default to LoRA training type.

from_checkpoint.description: The checkpoint identifier to continue training from a previous fine-tuning job. Format is `{$JOB_ID}` or `{$OUTPUT_MODEL_NAME}` or `{$JOB_ID}:{$STEP}` or `{$OUTPUT_MODEL_NAME}:{$STEP}`. The step value is optional; without it, the final checkpoint will be used.

estimated_total_price.description: The price of the fine-tuning job

allowed_to_proceed.description: Whether the user is allowed to proceed with the fine-tuning job

user_limit.description: The user's credit limit in dollars

estimated_train_token_count.description: The estimated number of tokens to be trained

estimated_eval_token_count.description: The estimated number of tokens for evaluation

message.description: Error message explaining the model is not available.

display_name.description: A human-readable name for the endpoint

state.description: The desired state of the endpoint

inactive_timeout.description: The number of minutes of inactivity after which the endpoint will be automatically stopped. Set to 0 to disable automatic timeout.

status.description: The status of the evaluation job

results.description: The results of the evaluation job

value.description: Field name in the input data

ExamplePayload.description: Placeholder schema for path example refs
